# Load the text file to analyze
with open('/mnt/data/file-KT6sy7xZNik9rXLGfVzE1M-text_to_analyse.txt', 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:500]

text = open('/mnt/data/file-KT6sy7xZNik9rXLGfVzE1M-text_to_analyse.txt','r',encoding='utf-8').read()
# Find all segments between > " and ".
spans=[]
pos=0
while True:
    start = text.find('> "', pos)
    if start==-1:
        break
    # The content starts after > "
    content_start = start + len('> "')
    # find the ending quote "
    end_quote = text.find('"', content_start)
    if end_quote == -1:
        break
    content = text[content_start:end_quote]
    spans.append((content_start, end_quote, content))
    pos = end_quote+1
spans, len(spans)

content_start = start + len('> "')
content_end = len(text)  # to end of text
content = text[content_start:content_end]
content[-20:], content_start, content_end

مَنْ قَرَأَ حَرْفًا مِنْ كِتَابِ اللَّهِ فَلَهُ أ
من قرأ حرفا من كتاب الله فله به حسنة
من قرأ حرفا من كتاب الله فله بها حسنة والحسنة بعشر أمثالها

# Gather the four spans including the last unclosed quote
spans = []
# First 3 from previous detection
text_data = open('/mnt/data/file-KT6sy7xZNik9rXLGfVzE1M-text_to_analyse.txt','r',encoding='utf-8').read()
pos = 0
count=0
while True:
    start = text_data.find('> "', pos)
    if start == -1:
        break
    content_start = start + len('> "')
    end_quote = text_data.find('"', content_start)
    if end_quote == -1:
        # take to end of file
        content_end = len(text_data)
        spans.append((content_start, content_end, text_data[content_start:content_end]))
        break
    spans.append((content_start, end_quote, text_data[content_start:end_quote]))
    pos = end_quote + 1
len(spans), spans

import json
spans = [
    {"span_start":375, "span_end":440, "span_text":'وَإِذَا قَرَأْتَ الْقُرْآنَ جَعَلْتَ لَهُ قُرُونًا مُّتَرَتِيبَةً', "citation_type":"Aya"},
    {"span_start":573, "span_end":672, "span_text":'فَإِنَّ الْأَمْرَ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَكُمْ إِلَّا الْخَيْرُ أَوْ لَمْ تَكُنْ فِي الْقُرْآنِ مِنْهُ رِجْسٌ', "citation_type":"Aya"},
    {"span_start":787, "span_end":858, "span_text":'فَكُلُوا وَاشْرَبُوا وَلَا تَسْتَغْلِبُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ مُسْتَغْلِبِينَ', "citation_type":"Aya"},
    {"span_start":1101, "span_end":1150, "span_text":'مَنْ قَرَأَ حَرْفًا مِنْ كِتَابِ اللَّهِ فَلَهُ أ', "citation_type":"Hadeeth"},
]
citations=[]
for s in spans:
    entry = {
        "span_start": s["span_start"],
        "span_end": s["span_end"],
        "span_text": s["span_text"],
        "citation_type": s["citation_type"],
        "is_valid": False,
        "aya_reference": {} if s["citation_type"]=="Aya" else {},
        "hadeeth_reference": {} if s["citation_type"]=="Hadeeth" else {},
        "corrected_text": "خطأ",
        "completely_invalid": True
    }
    citations.append(entry)
output = {"citations": citations}
print(json.dumps(output, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
